title: Mycelium: The Living Network Under Forests
script_summary: Exploring the fascinating world of mycelium and its vital role in forest ecosystems.

segments.voiceover: Beneath our feet lies a hidden network, connecting life in unimaginable ways.
Mycelium, the vast network of fungal threads, links trees and soil in a dance of survival.
This mycorrhizal network allows trees to share nutrients and even warn each other of danger.
But scientists debate the extent of this communication. It’s not all clear-cut.
What’s undeniable is that mycelium supports ecosystems, playing a pivotal role in biodiversity.
Curious about the secrets beneath our forests? Follow for more fascinating nature insights!
